title: First INC Session (1885)
provisions: Held at: Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College, Bombay (28 December 1885)
No specific decisions mentioned beyond formation

title: INC Session (1907)
provisions: Initially planned at Nagpur but shifted to Surat
Fierce altercation between Moderates and Extremists
Led to the split in the Congress

title: INC Session (1916)
provisions: Resulted in the merger of Extremists and Moderates
For the first time Muslim League participated in a Congress session
Lucknow Pact signed: Congress accepted separate electorate for Muslims
One-third representation for Muslims in the Central Legislative Council

title: INC Session (1917)
provisions: Annie Besant became the first woman President of the Congress

title: Special INC Session (1920)
provisions: Opposed by C.R. Das, Lala Lajpat Rai, Bipin Chandra Pal, Annie Besant and Muhammad Ali
Final decision deferred to the Annual INC Session

title: Annual INC Session (1920)
provisions: Creation of the Congress Working Committee (CWC)
Provincial Congress Committees on linguistic basis
Formation of All-India Congress Committee (AICC) to coordinate provinces
Promotion of Hindi (Devanagari script) for inter-provincial contact
Strengthening Congress at the grassroots level
Reduction of Congress membership fee to 25 paise
Non-Cooperation Programme: Boycott of British goods, government services, public institutions, judiciary, schools, colleges and titles
Promotion of made-in-India goods and new educational institutions
Promotion of Khadi and distribution of Khadi and Charkha among the masses
Gandhi and C.R. Das resolved their differences

title: INC Annual Session (1922)
provisions: Pro-Changers (C.R. Das, Motilal Nehru) favoured ending boycott of councils and entry into Provincial and Legislative Councils
No-Changers (Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, C. Rajagopalachari, Rajendra Prasad) opposed council entry
No-Changers supported constructive work as suggested by Gandhi

title: INC Annual Session (1924)
provisions: For the first time Gandhi became Congress President

title: All India Congress Committee Meeting (1927)
provisions: Congress must aim for Poorna Swaraj instead of self-rule

title: INC Annual Session (1929)
provisions: Poorna Swaraj adopted as Congress objective
Congress decision to boycott 1st Round Table Conference
Gandhi authorised to launch the Civil Disobedience Movement
26 January 1930: Tri-colour flag unfurled on the banks of River Ravi
26 January 1930 observed as Poorna Swaraj Day

title: INC Annual Session (1931)
provisions: Purna Swaraj explained for the masses
Political freedom to include economic freedom
Gandhi permitted to participate in the Second Round Table Conference

title: INC Session (1936)
provisions: Congress rejected Government of India Act 1935

title: INC Session (1937)
provisions: No discussion on abolition of zamindari

title: INC Session (1938)
provisions: Proposed creation of a National Planning Committee
Jawaharlal Nehru appointed Chairman of Planning Committee
Bose expressed reservations about Gandhi's charkha policy
Bose expressed reservations about Gandhian village-centric development model

title: INC Session (1939)
provisions: Bose defeated Dr. Pattabhi Sitaramayya in presidential election

title: INC Session (1940)
provisions: Presided by: Maulana Abul Kalam Azad
India as an enslaved nation cannot support war effectively
